tweets.text:
The 'sticky' app formula:

1. Daily use case
2. Network effects (more friends = more value)
3. Variable rewards (unpredictable dopamine)
4. Social obligation (FOMO if you don't check)

TBH/Gas had all 4.

Philippines: $38B remittances annually (9% of GDP).

Average cost: 5.8%.

Stablecoin adoption growing 40% YoY.

When your economy depends on remittances, you optimize for what works.

CBDC retail vs wholesale:

- Wholesale: Banks only (faster settlement)
- Retail: Everyone (complex, political)

Most countries doing wholesale first.
Retail CBDCs are hard. Really hard.

Smart contracts are 'trustless' - but:

- Code can have bugs
- Oracles can be manipulated
- Admin keys can be compromised

Trustless doesn't mean riskless. It means different risks.

DAOs in practice:

- Promise: Decentralized governance
- Reality: Low voter turnout
- Reality: Whales dominate votes
- Reality: Most proposals technical/boring

Governance is hard. Decentralized governance is harder.

Bitcoin halving April 2024:

- Block reward: 6.25 → 3.125 BTC
- New supply: ~900 → ~450 BTC/day
- Historical: Price often rises post-halving
- Mechanism: Reduced selling pressure

Supply mechanics matter.

Compliment apps keep getting built.
Most fail.

Why? They copy TBH's features but not its timing.

TBH 2017: Instagram fatigue, no competition
TBH 2024: TikTok dominates, market saturated

Context is everything.

Turkey: 65% inflation.

Stablecoin volume on local exchanges: Record highs.

When your lira loses half its value in a year, you don't need to understand crypto. You just need to survive.

Building viral: The invitation matters more than the product.

Bad invite: 'Download my app'
Good invite: 'Someone said something nice about you'

TBH's invites had 80% open rate. Context > Content.

Brazil's Drex (CBDC):

- Launch: 2024 pilot
- Unique feature: Programmable money
- Use case: Conditional payments
- Innovation: Smart contracts on CBDC

Pushing boundaries beyond simple payments.
